channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts head for home, wrapping up final tests and experiments - CBS News
item.description:
1. Artemis II astronauts head for home, wrapping up final tests and experiments  CBS News

  4. Photos: NASA releases first images from moon flyby  NPR
  5. NASA Releases Photos of Far Side of the Moon From Artemis II Astronauts  The New York Times

item.title: 'Audible screams of delight' from NASA scientists over micrometeorite impacts on the moon witnessed by Artemis 2 astronauts - Space
item.description:
1. 'Audible screams of delight' from NASA scientists over micrometeorite impacts on the moon witnessed by Artemis 2 astronauts  Space

  3. 'Pinprick of light': Artemis crew witnesses meteorite impacts on moon  Phys.org
  4. NASA’s Moon ship and rocket seem to be working well, so what about the landers?  Ars Technica
  5. NASA’s Artemis II Astronauts Spot Mysterious New Lunar Features During Historic Mission  The Debrief

item.title: Meteor that streaked over N.J., other eastern states, was going 30,000 mph - NJ.com

item.description:

item.title: 'Oldest octopus' fossil is no octopus at all, scans reveal - Phys.org
item.description:

  3. The ‘oldest fossil octopus’ is probably another animal  Science News
  4. Remarkable discovery inside museum item after 26 years: 'Stopped us in our tracks'  Yahoo News Australia
  5. So long, sucker: world’s ‘oldest octopus’ is stripped of its title  The Times

item.description:

item.title: Artemis II’s astronauts are on their way home—a six-figure salary but no overtime or hazard pay awaits them back on Earth - Fortune
item.description:
1. Artemis II’s astronauts are on their way home—a six-figure salary but no overtime or hazard pay awaits them back on Earth  Fortune
  2. The Artemis II astronauts are flying to the moon — here’s what they’re actually getting paid  NJ.com
  3. How much will the Artemis II astronauts get paid for their trip around the moon?  KING5.com
  4. Reid Wiseman Or Christina Kock, Who's The Richest Artemis II Astronaut? Know Their Net Worths  bollywoodshaadis.com
  5. Artemis II mission, how much is the salary of the astronauts traveling around the Moon?  Balkanweb.com

item.title: 'Bones all over': UT paleontologist finds 100,000-year-old fossils in Central Texas cave - Austin American-Statesman
item.description:
1. 'Bones all over': UT paleontologist finds 100,000-year-old fossils in Central Texas cave  Austin American-Statesman
  2. Fossils discovered in Central Texas cave could shed new light on prehistoric life in the area  kcentv.com
  3. Texas researcher uncovers rare prehistoric fossil site in underwater cave  kare11.com
  4. 'It blew my mind': Long-lost ice-age ecosystem, including fossils of lion-size armadillo and giant ground sloth, discovered in Texas 'water cave'  Live Science
  5. Word Wrangler: April 8, 2026  Texas Monthly

item.title: Artemis 2 astronauts spy the Milky Way photo of the day for April 8, 2026 - Space
item.description:
1. Artemis 2 astronauts spy the Milky Way photo of the day for April 8, 2026  Space
  2. Why you can’t see space junk in Artemis II photos  Popular Science
  3. NASA Shares "Heavenly View" Of Milky Way Taken By Artemis II Crew  NDTV
  4. Yes, You Can See Stars And Planets In The Artemis II Photos – Here's Why  IFLScience
  5. Why you can’t see any stars in photos from Artemis II  The Hill
